skill.name: learning-objectives
skill.description: Generate measurable learning objectives for curriculum, lessons, and assessments using Bloom's taxonomy, CEFR levels, prerequisites, success criteria, and assessment alignment.
skill.summary: Create measurable, standards-aligned learning objectives for educational planning.
skill.author: 92Bilal26

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk weak-crypto, C2 keyword, reconnaissance, and Ruby backtick patterns. Review found these are educational prose, Markdown examples, taxonomy terms, and a local Python validator, with no malicious intent, network access, credential handling, subprocess execution, or prompt-injection attempt. Residual risk is low because the skill includes a script that reads a user-supplied YAML file for validation.

low_findings.title: Static High-Risk Keyword Matches Are Educational Prose
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography, C2 keyword, system reconnaissance, and network reconnaissance alerts are false positives. The cited content discusses Bloom's taxonomy, CEFR proficiency mapping, prerequisite chains, assessment wording, and educational examples rather than cryptography, command-and-control behavior, or reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain plain educational guidance and examples. No cryptographic primitive, beaconing logic, system probing, or network operation is present in these contexts.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Alerts Do Not Indicate Shell Injection
low_findings.description: The static Ruby backtick findings are Markdown code fences and inline examples. The only command instruction asks the assistant to run the bundled Python validator on an objectives file; the validator itself does not invoke a shell or subprocess.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited Markdown regions are fenced examples, and the Python script prints validation output directly. There is no os.system, subprocess call, eval, or dynamic command construction.

low_findings.title: Local Validator Reads a User-Supplied YAML Path
low_findings.description: The validation script accepts a file path argument, checks that the file exists, and loads it with yaml.safe_load. This is a limited local filesystem read used for validation, with no network transmission or shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file-read behavior is direct and confirmed, but the user controls the target path and the parser uses safe_load. The impact is limited unless a user intentionally points it at sensitive local data.

content.value_statement: Educators often need objectives that are specific, measurable, and aligned with assessment. This skill turns topics into structured outcomes with Bloom levels, CEFR progression, prerequisites, and success criteria.
actual_capabilities: Drafts measurable learning objectives from a topic, learner level, timeframe, and end goal.
Maps objectives to Bloom's taxonomy levels from Remember through Create.
Adds CEFR-style proficiency levels for progressive learner development.
Identifies prerequisites that learners should know before starting a topic.
Pairs objectives with assessment methods and success criteria.
Provides a local validator for objective measurability and required fields.
limitations: It does not replace accreditation review or institutional curriculum approval.
Generated objectives still need educator review for local standards and learner context.
The validator checks structure and wording, not teaching effectiveness in practice.
The skill focuses on education design and does not create full lesson materials by itself.

use_cases.title: Plan a Technical Lesson
use_cases.description: Create three to five measurable objectives for a programming lesson with assessments and success criteria.

use_cases.title: Align a Course Module
use_cases.description: Map module outcomes to Bloom levels, CEFR progression, prerequisite knowledge, and evidence of mastery.

use_cases.title: Improve Existing Outcomes
use_cases.description: Rewrite vague goals into testable learning objectives with clear verbs and assessment methods.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Objectives
prompt_templates.prompt: Create learning objectives for a beginner lesson on [topic]. The learners are [audience], the lesson lasts [time], and the end goal is [goal]. Include Bloom level, context, assessment method, and success criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Rewrite Vague Goals
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite these learning goals into measurable objectives: [paste goals]. Replace vague verbs, add learner actions, and include assessment evidence for each objective.

prompt_templates.title: Map Prerequisites and Progression
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a progression of learning objectives for [topic] from foundational knowledge to independent application. Include prerequisites, Bloom levels, CEFR levels, and assessment methods.

prompt_templates.title: Align Objectives to Evals
prompt_templates.prompt: Given these success evals and business goals: [paste evals], create aligned learning objectives. Show how each objective maps to an eval, assessment method, and success criterion.

output_examples.input: Create objectives for a one-hour lesson on Python list comprehensions for beginners.
output: Define list comprehension syntax and identify its main parts.
Explain how a list comprehension replaces a simple for loop.
Implement list comprehensions that filter and transform small data sets.
Compare a list comprehension with a loop using readability and correctness criteria.

output_examples.input: Improve this goal: Students will understand REST APIs.
output_examples.output: Revised objective: Explain the request-response flow of a REST API and identify method, path, status code, and response body in a provided example.

output_examples.input: Create assessment criteria for an objective about class design.
output: The class models the requested domain accurately.
Attributes store required state with clear names.
Methods perform the required behavior and return expected results.
The learner justifies design choices using maintainability criteria.

best_practices: Start from the final assessment or success eval before writing objectives.
Use specific action verbs that can be observed or assessed.
Limit objectives to the essential outcomes for the available teaching time.
anti_patterns: Do not use vague verbs such as know, learn, or understand without observable evidence.
Do not create more objectives than the lesson can realistically assess.
Do not skip prerequisites when learners need foundations to succeed.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates measurable learning objectives with Bloom levels, CEFR levels, prerequisites, assessments, and success criteria.

faq.question: Can it review existing objectives?
faq.answer: Yes. It can rewrite vague objectives into clearer outcomes with observable learner actions.

faq.question: Does it support programming education?
faq.answer: Yes. The reference materials include programming examples for Bloom levels, prerequisites, and assessment methods.

faq.question: Does it generate full lessons?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on outcomes and assessment alignment, which can guide later lesson design.

faq.question: What is the validator used for?
faq.answer: The validator checks whether objectives have measurable verbs, context, assessment methods, and success criteria.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
